title: HX Stomp Block Catalog
description: Schema for the HX Stomp effects block catalog. Validates the top-level catalog document and every block entry within it.

catalog_version.description: Semantic version of this catalog schema/data (e.g. '1.0.0').

firmware_version.description: HX Stomp firmware version this catalog targets (e.g. '3.80').

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date the catalog was last modified (e.g. '2026-03-21').

dsp_disclaimer.description: Human-readable disclaimer explaining that DSP cost values are community-derived estimates, not official Line 6 figures.

blocks.description: Flat array of all HX Stomp effect blocks. Includes HX (Mono/Stereo) and Legacy models.

Block.description: A single HX Stomp effects block.

id.description: Globally unique snake_case identifier for this block (e.g. 'dist_kinky_boost'). Used as a stable key in app logic.

name.description: Display name of the block exactly as it appears in the HX Stomp model list (e.g. 'Kinky Boost').

category.description: Top-level block category as shown in the HX Stomp model list.

hx_subcategory.description: Subcategory as shown in the HX Stomp hardware model list. Reflects the device's own menu taxonomy.

subcategory.description: Descriptive functional subcategory for app-side filtering and display (e.g. 'Boost', 'Phaser', 'Analog Delay'). Not tied to the hardware menu.

based_on.description: The specific real-world hardware this block models (e.g. 'Klon Centaur'). Null for Line 6 Original designs.

description.description: Human-readable description of the block's character, behavior, and typical use cases.

real_life_alternative.description: Suggested real-world pedal(s) a player could use to achieve a similar sound. May list multiple options. Null if no clear analogue exists.

parameters.description: Ordered list of parameters for this block, matching the order they appear on the HX Stomp display.

DspCost.description: Community-estimated DSP cost for this block. See top-level dsp_disclaimer.

mono.description: Estimated DSP cost when running in mono. Null if the block cannot run in mono.

stereo.description: Estimated DSP cost when running in stereo. Null if no stereo variant exists for this block.

confidence.description: How reliable the DSP cost figures are. 'measured' = corroborated by community DSP meter observation. 'estimated' = derived by relative comparison. 'unknown' = no reliable data available.

notes.description: Any additional context about DSP cost for this block (e.g. mono-only, unusually expensive, legacy port).

IO.description: Input/output routing configuration for this block.

routing.description: Signal routing capability of this block.

inputs.description: Number of audio inputs the block accepts.

outputs.description: Number of audio outputs the block produces.

Parameter.description: A single adjustable parameter on an HX Stomp block.

name.description: Parameter name exactly as it appears on the HX Stomp display (e.g. 'Feedback', 'Low Freq').

unit.description: Unit of measurement for this parameter (e.g. 'dB', 'Hz', 'ms', '%', 'semitones', ':1'). Null for dimensionless parameters (Q factor, 0–10 knobs) and toggle/enum types.

min.description: Minimum value. Null for enum and toggle types where a numeric range is not applicable.

max.description: Maximum value. Null for enum and toggle types where a numeric range is not applicable.

default.description: Default value as set by the HX Stomp firmware when the block is first instantiated. Number for continuous/toggle types, string for enum types.

step.description: Minimum increment between valid values. Null for enum types. Use 1 for integer-only parameters.

type.description: Control type. 'continuous' = knob or slider with a numeric range. 'toggle' = binary on/off switch. 'enum' = multi-option selector (waveform, mode, key, scale, etc.).

options.description: Required when type is 'toggle' or 'enum'. Lists all valid named values in display order.

visible_when.description: Conditional visibility rule. Null means the parameter is always visible. When present, the parameter is only shown when a named sibling parameter matches a specified value or array of values.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what the parameter does, its perceptual effect, and any practical tips.

VisibilityCondition.description: Defines when a parameter should be visible based on the current value of a sibling parameter.

param.description: The name of the sibling parameter whose value controls visibility (e.g. 'Voice 1 Mode').

value.description: The value (or array of values) the controlling parameter must equal for this parameter to be visible.
